title: Lake of the Ozarks Boat Rental: Costs, Seasons & Best Boats 2026
html:
Lake of the Ozarks in Missouri offers some of the finest boating in the Midwest, with miles of shoreline and countless coves to explore. A half-day small motorboat rental from a marina like Camden on the Lake typically costs from USD 250. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to secure your ideal vessel.

Where to set off in Lake of the Ozarks

Popular departure points include the Lake of the Ozarks State Park marina, the Camden on the Lake resort marina, and the community docks at Osage Beach and Sunrise Beach. Miller Cove and Shawnee Bend provide quieter launch ramps. Further north, the Gravois Arm offers access to the 19-mile-long Niangua Arm. Each marina has its own character and parking facilities. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Pontoon boat, ideal for relaxed family cruising on the main channel, seating up to 12 guests, from USD 350 per half-day.
  2. Bowrider speedboat, perfect for waterskiing and wakeboarding, carries up to 8 guests, from USD 280 for two hours.
  3. Cabin cruiser, suited for overnight trips or longer explorations, sleeps 4 to 6 guests, from USD 600 per day.
  4. Deck boat, a versatile choice for combining sunbathing and tubing, holds up to 10 guests, from USD 320 per half-day.
  5. Power catamaran, spacious and stable for large groups, capacity up to 14 guests, from USD 800 per day.

For example, the Limpopo for hire is a 28-foot Axopar 28 that sleeps two and includes a toilet; it is ideal for couples wanting a sporty day out. Check availability on its listing page to see detailed specifications and calendar. One option worth a look is the Limpopo for hire.

What are the boat rental costs at Lake of the Ozarks?

  • Shared guided tour per person: from USD 60 for a two-hour sightseeing trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from USD 250, excluding fuel.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from USD 1,200, including captain.
  • Crewed luxury yacht per day: from USD 3,000, with full crew and catering.

Prices vary by season and boat age. For the best choice, you can find a skippered charter on the platform, which often includes fuel and insurance. You can also see more at check availability.

When is the best season to rent a boat at Lake of the Ozarks?

High season runs from May through September, with July and August being the busiest. The prevailing south-westerly wind can make the main channel choppy in the afternoon, so morning trips are often calmer.

Spring and autumn offer milder weather and fewer crowds. Late September still provides warm water and sunny days, making it an excellent choice for relaxed cruising. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat at Lake of the Ozarks?

Missouri requires anyone born after 1 January 1984 to hold a boating safety certification to operate a motorised vessel. Rental operators will confirm the exact requirements for your situation, and many offer a brief on-water orientation. If you book through BoatHire24, each listing specifies whether a licence or briefing is needed, and you can check the booking FAQ for common questions about documentation and deposits.

At a glance

  • Price band: USD 60, 3,000 per day
  • Peak months: May, September
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 2, 14 persons
  • Currency: USD

Questions about boat rental at Lake of the Ozarks

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours at Lake of the Ozarks?

Yes, many operators offer two-hour rentals for speedboats and half-day bookings for pontoons and deck boats.

Are there any age restrictions for driving a rental boat?

Most operators require the driver to be at least 21 years old and to hold a valid driving licence, with additional limits for those born after 1983.

Is fuel included in the rental price?

Fuel is typically not included; you pay for what you use, and some marinas require a full tank return.

Can I dock the rental boat at different marinas?

Many rental agreements allow docking at other public marinas along the lake, but confirm with the operator beforehand to avoid extra fees.

To begin your booking, please check availability directly on the site. If you own a boat, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of potential renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake of the Ozarks and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
